sources.notes: Count subsequences preserving the set of visible record heights from both left and right under easy constraints.
Official editorial lists the same structural solution; the hard-version reference code computes left and right record-preserving counts and combines them at a maximum element.

annotation.summary: Count subsequences whose left-visible and right-visible tower height sets are the same as in the original array.
annotation.constraints: The easy version has small enough limits for direct DP over the visible-record sequence.
annotation.core_idea: The maximum height must be selected, and it splits the subsequence into a left part that preserves the left record-height sequence and a right part that preserves the right record-height sequence. Dynamic programming counts ways to scan the array and match each required record while optionally taking elements that do not create a new record. Combining the counts over choices of the maximum position gives the answer.
annotation.complexity: O(n * |L| + n * |R|) in the easy DP formulation.

solution_variants.name: record-sequence-dp
solution_variants.summary: Compute DP counts for subsequences preserving the left and right record sequences, then combine at selected maximum positions.

tags.evidence: The solution counts subsequences by DP over progress in the required visible-record sequence.
The task is to count index-subsequences satisfying left/right visibility constraints.
The preserved objects are exactly prefix maxima from the left and suffix maxima from the right.
